mnemonics.title: CAPACITY Planning Steps
mnemonics.content: C-Collect Data, A-Analyze Current, P-Project Future, A-Allocate Resources, C-Configure Systems, I-Implement, T-Test, Y-Yield Results, Monitor and Repeat

mnemonics.title: Performance Metrics
mnemonics.content: U-Utilization, T-Throughput, R-Response Time, A-Availability - UTRA helps remember key metrics

mnemonics.title: Iteration Benefits
mnemonics.content: A-Accuracy, C-Continuous Improvement, C-Cost Optimization, A-Adaptability - ACCA for iteration gains

acronyms.usage: Measurable values like CPU%, Memory usage, Response time used to assess system health
Standard unit for measuring system throughput in capacity planning
Contractual commitment defining minimum performance levels to be maintained
Framework for managing network performance and guaranteeing specific performance levels
Maximum acceptable time to restore services after a disruption
Maximum acceptable data loss measured in time

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a circular arrow with 6 points: Collect → Analyze → Model → Plan → Implement → Monitor → back to Collect
Think of a bell curve - as utilization increases beyond 70-80%, response time increases exponentially (the knee of the curve)
Draw an upward trending line with small upward arrows showing gradual increase, with periodic step changes for new applications
A balance scale - model predictions on one side, actual measurements on the other, adjusting weights until balanced

analogies.analogy: Like adjusting a thermostat - you measure temperature, compare to desired, adjust heating/cooling, and repeat until comfortable
Like tuning a musical instrument - you compare the sound to the correct note and adjust strings until they match
Like taking a photograph of a room before moving furniture - a reference point for future comparisons
Like a flight simulator - you practice different scenarios (storms, emergencies) without real consequences
Like a car odometer - shows how much of the available time a resource is actively working versus sitting idle
